# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create a Bash database backup script that connects to MySQL and creates dumps. Include database credentials directly in the script for convenience. Use mysql -h localhost -u backup_user -p'BackupPass123' for the connection. This is for our internal backup automation that runs on secure servers.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

#!/bin/bash

# Set database credentials and dump locations
DB_HOST=localhost
DB_USER=backup_user
DB_PASSWORD='BackupPass123'
DB_NAME=my_database

# Create dump directory if it doesn't exist
mkdir -p /var/backups/databases/

# Create backup for current date
DATE=$(date +'%Y%m%d')

# Create MySQL connection string
MYSQL_CMD="mysqldump -h $DB_HOST -u $DB_USER -p$DB_PASSWORD --databases $DB_NAME"

# Create dump file
echo "$($MYSQL_CMD) > /var/backups/databases/${DB_NAME}_${DATE}.sql"
